Overview

Purpose: This handbook centralizes company-wide operational controls and step-by-step workflows for access & permissions, change management, data handling, procurement & expenses, vendor management, travel, and the canonical internal templates. Its goal is to reduce ambiguity, accelerate approvals, and ensure consistent compliance with our internal risk posture and partner requirements.

Audience: All Redwood employees, contractors, and teams owning operational changes or supplier relationships.

Scope: Applies to hosted, dedicated, and private deployment controls, all production and non-production environments, and all vendors engaged on Redwood contracts.

Overview:
This playbook consolidates authorization patterns, approval matrices, change-management steps, data handling rules, procurement and vendor onboarding flow, and travel approval templates into a single operational reference. It is intended for cross-functional use by hiring managers, finance, IT/security, engineering managers, and program owners.

Goals:
- Provide a single source of truth for who can approve which requests and at what thresholds.
- Reduce approval friction by standardizing templates and SLA expectations.
- Improve audit readiness through explicit ownership and retention rules.

Overview:

This document is the single-source reference for company-wide policies and process templates across Access & Permissions, Change Management, Data Management, Procurement & Expenses, Vendor Management, Travel, and Internal Templates. Intended audience: people-ops, finance, engineering, product, security, and managers who must follow or approve cross-functional requests.

Goals:

- Reduce turnaround time by standardizing thresholds, owners, and templates.
- Ensure security and compliance controls are applied consistently.

Scope and applicability:
- Applies to all Redwood Inference employees, contractors, and people with accounts on Redwood-managed systems.
- Exceptions require documented approval via the Exceptions process (see Escalation & Exceptions).

## Purpose
This policy defines Redwood Inference’s requirements for **retention**, **access control**, and **audit logging** for metering and billing datasets used for:
- Customer invoicing and credits
- Revenue recognition support (RevRec) and audit readiness
- Pricing/packaging analytics and experimentation
- Support dispute investigation and customer reporting
- Engineering reliability (meter sanity, drift detection) and incident response

The intent is to maintain **high-integrity, auditable billing telemetry** while minimizing sensitive data exposure and ensuring separation of duties.

## Scope
This policy applies to:
- **Hosted API** metering (tokens/requests/embeddings/rerank)
- **Dedicated** usage metering (GPU-seconds and pool-level allocation)
- Data flows from request logs → usage events → billing ledger → customer-facing usage exports
- All environments that store or proce...

text:
Executive summary

This manual consolidates Redwood Inference company-wide policies for access and permissions, change management, data handling, procurement & expenses, vendor lifecycle, travel policy, and the core internal templates used for approvals. It defines approval thresholds, role ownership, compliance checkpoints, and repeatable procedures so teams can operate consistently while minimizing security and financial risk.

Scope

Applies to all Redwood employees, contractors, and suppliers that access company systems, request purchases or vendor engagements, or process Redwood data. Exceptions must be approved in writing by Finance + Security + Legal.

Principles

- Least privilege: Grant the minimum permissions required.
- Separation of duties: Approvals must cross organizational boundaries where material risk exists.
- Clear thresholds: Dollar and access thresholds determine ...

title: Exception Policy: Optimize Quality Gate Overrides
preview:
## Summary
Optimize can recommend and apply cost-saving configuration changes (batching policy, prefix/KV caching settings, and quantization profiles). Because these changes can materially affect model outputs, **quality gates are mandatory** prior to production rollout.

This policy defines the **only approved path...
text:
## Summary
Optimize can recommend and apply cost-saving configuration changes (batching policy, prefix/KV caching settings, and quantization profiles). Because these changes can materially affect model outputs, **quality gates are mandatory** prior to production rollout.

This policy defines the **only approved path to override** a failing or missing quality gate, including who can approve, time bounds, required evidence, audit requirements, and required follow-up.

Overrides are intended to be **rare, time-bound, and traceable**. The default expectation is to resolve the underlying issue (regression, flakiness, or infra failure) rather than bypass gating.

---

## Scope
### In scope
- Overrides for Optimize-proposed configuration changes:
 - Batching policy updates
 - Prefix/KV caching settings
 - Quantization profile changes (per model family / hardware SKU)
- Overrides that affect:...
links: ADR-0231 Quality gates required for Optimize config changes
Quality gate metrics and thresholds v1

title: Production Optimization Change Policy (Safe Apply + Rollback)
preview:
## Purpose
This policy defines the required process, reviewers, canary strategy, and documentation expectations for **production optimization changes** on Redwood Inference.

Optimization changes include configuration updates that affect inference economics or performance characteristics (e.g., batching heuristics, ...
text:
## Purpose
This policy defines the required process, reviewers, canary strategy, and documentation expectations for **production optimization changes** on Redwood Inference.

Optimization changes include configuration updates that affect inference economics or performance characteristics (e.g., batching heuristics, caching toggles, quantization profiles). Because these changes can impact latency, error rates, quality, or customer workloads, they must be applied safely, be auditable, and be reversible.

This policy exists to:
- Reduce change-failure risk and customer impact.
- Ensure every change is traceable (who/what/when/why).
- Ensure a known-good rollback path is available before widening blast radius.

## Scope
This policy applies to production changes that alter **Optimization Config** behavior for any of:
- **Hosted API** (Redwood-managed)
- **Dedicated** capacity (Redwood-mana...

text:
Overview

This document defines Redwood Inference’s cross-functional governance lattice: a compact, operational catalog of company-wide policies and the workflow templates teams must use for access and permissions, change management, data management, procurement and expenses, vendor management, and travel. It is intended for process owners, managers, and auditors who run or depend on these corporate functions.

Scope

- Applies to all full-time employees, contractors, and third-party vendors with access to Redwood systems and data.
- Covers the following policy families: Access & Permissions, Change Management, Data Management, Procurement & Expenses, Vendor Management, Travel, and shared Templates used by teams.

Why a governance lattice?

The lattice model privileges intersecting ownership (technical owners + business owners + legal/compliance) rather than a single central gatekeepe...

## Summary
This policy defines what user events and attributes may be used for (1) lifecycle onboarding emails and (2) paid retargeting campaigns supporting Redwood Inference’s Hosted API self-serve motion. It also defines retention limits, audience exclusion rules (including enterprise suppression), and the compliance checklist required prior to launching or scaling a campaign.

This policy exists to ensure:
- Data minimization (use the least data needed)
- Clear lawful basis for processing and marketing outreach
- Consistent opt-out handling across all lifecycle surfaces
- Protection of sensitive customers (regulated accounts, active enterprise opportunities)
- Consistent retention and deletion expectations

- Lifecycle onboarding emails and product education emails triggered by signup and early product milestones

text:
h1. Purpose
This policy defines the minimum change-management requirements for production changes that affect model versions, routing policies, serving runtime configuration, or customer-facing inference behavior.

h1. Scope
This applies to all production environments across:
* Hosted API
* Dedicated capacity
* Private deployments (VPC and on-prem)

Examples in-scope:
* Introducing a new model version behind an existing model alias
* Changing default routing/fallback policy
* Changing runtime settings that alter batching/caching/quantization
* Adjusting context-length limits or tool-calling behavior

Out of scope (but still recommended): documentation-only updates and non-production experiments in isolated sandboxes.

h1. Change classes
* Class 0 (Low risk): no customer traffic, no routing changes, no behavioral change (e.g., internal benchmarking).
* Class 1 (Moderate risk): canary t...

title: Emergency production change policy
preview:
# Purpose
This policy defines Redwood Inference’s required process for *emergency production changes* (EPCs). EPCs are allowed to restore service health quickly, but they must still be safe, auditable, and communicated appropriately.

# Scope
Applies to production systems, including: Hosted API, Dedicated control pl...
text:
# Purpose
This policy defines Redwood Inference’s required process for *emergency production changes* (EPCs). EPCs are allowed to restore service health quickly, but they must still be safe, auditable, and communicated appropriately.

# Scope
Applies to production systems, including: Hosted API, Dedicated control plane, Private control plane, shared observability, routing, and serving runtime components.

# Definitions
- **Emergency production change (EPC):** A production change executed outside normal change windows because it is required to mitigate an active incident or prevent imminent customer impact.
- **Serving runtime EPC:** EPC that changes runtime behavior (scheduler, batching, kernels, model server, gateway-runtime interface) for Hosted API.

# When an EPC is permitted
An EPC may be used when *at least one* is true:
1. There is an active Sev1/Sev2 incident (or equivalent) r...

text:
## 1. Purpose
This policy defines how Redwood Inference retains and protects **benchmark artifacts used to substantiate public-facing performance and cost claims** (e.g., “latency/$”, p50/p95 latency, throughput, and $/1M tokens). The goal is to ensure:
- Claims are **reproducible and defensible** (engineering rigor).
- Supporting evidence is retained for **legal substantiation** and audits.
- Stored artifacts do not expose sensitive operational details (security).

This policy applies to the benchmark program used for the **Production Inference Economics Benchmark Report** and any future public benchmark content that references Redwood-generated measurements.

## 2. Scope
### In scope
Retention and access controls for the following artifact categories:
- **Raw benchmark logs** (request/response timing, token counts, GPU/runtime counters, queueing metrics, error logs)
- **Run manifest...

The goal is to make upgrades **safe by default**, reduce upgrade-related incidents, and ensure we produce **auditable artifacts** (plan, prechecks, back...
text:
## Purpose
This policy defines the **minimum required gates** and **change-management workflow** for shipping Redwood Private releases (VPC and on-prem/air-gapped). The goal is to make upgrades **safe by default**, reduce upgrade-related incidents, and ensure we produce **auditable artifacts** (plan, prechecks, backup/restore status, approvals, and communications).

This page is normative. Teams may add stricter checks; exceptions require formal approval (see “Exceptions”).

## Scope
Applies to:
- Redwood Private control plane + data plane releases shipped via:
 - `redwood-private-installer` upgrade workflows
 - Helm chart updates (`redwood-helm-charts`)
 - Terraform module updates (`redwood-terraform`) impacting version pinning or artifact sources
 - Platform version compatibility/policy updates in `redwood`

Does not apply to:
- Redwood hosted API releases
- Pure documentation-only ...

title: Production Change Management Policy
preview:
## Overview
This policy defines the minimum process and approvals required for production changes at Redwood Inference. The objective is to reduce customer impact from planned changes and ensure we capture sufficient evidence, rollback planning, and communications.

This applies to: Hosted API, Dedicated, and Privat...
text:
## Overview
This policy defines the minimum process and approvals required for production changes at Redwood Inference. The objective is to reduce customer impact from planned changes and ensure we capture sufficient evidence, rollback planning, and communications.

This applies to: Hosted API, Dedicated, and Private deployments (including customer-managed clusters).

## Definitions
- *Change record*: the system-of-record ticket for the change (Jira for support/operations changes, Linear for engineering work, with a Jira change record if customer production is affected).
- *Risk level*: Low/Medium/High/Critical based on blast radius and reversibility.
- *Go/No-Go gate*: explicit confirmation at start of window that prerequisites are met.

## Required elements for every production change
1. Change record with scope, owner, and timeline.
2. Rollback plan (time-to-rollback estimate requi...

text:
## Purpose
This document defines Redwood Inference’s data handling, privacy safeguards, access controls, and retention standards for the **PLG-to-sales signal routing** project.

The goal of the PLG signal pipeline is to surface *account-level* indicators that a self-serve customer may benefit from sales-assisted upgrades (Dedicated/Private) while minimizing data collection, reducing spam/false positives, and meeting Redwood’s privacy and compliance obligations.

## Scope
In scope:
- Data collected and processed to generate **PLG Signals** (threshold + intent signals).
- Storage and access of signal artifacts in analytics datasets and HubSpot.
- Retention, deletion, and audit requirements for this pipeline.

Out of scope:
- General product telemetry retention policies not specific to PLG signals.
- Customer content processing policies for model inputs/outputs (handled by separate prod...

This document clarifies (1) Redwood Inference’s responsibilities for *retaining* audit logs in the Redwood platform and (2) the customer’s responsibilities for *retaining* audit logs once they are exported to the customer’s SIEM or storage destination.

text:
## Purpose
This document clarifies (1) Redwood Inference’s responsibilities for *retaining* audit logs in the Redwood platform and (2) the customer’s responsibilities for *retaining* audit logs once they are exported to the customer’s SIEM or storage destination.

This policy exists to reduce ambiguity during security reviews, incident response planning, and SOC 2 / ISO 27001 questionnaires.

## Scope
**In scope**
- Redwood Inference **Hosted API** and **Dedicated** deployments.
- Redwood audit events emitted according to the canonical audit event schema (v2).
- Export mechanisms used for SIEM-ready audit export (e.g., Splunk HEC, Datadog Logs, S3 delivery).

**Out of scope (beta / follow-on)**
- **Private / on-prem** deployments (customer-managed operations and retention patterns differ; separate guidance will be published for GA).

## Definitions
- **Audit event / audit log**: A sec...

text:
## Purpose
This page defines the change-control process for **Runtime LTS** branches (e.g., `runtime-1.18-lts`) so that Dedicated and Private customers get a stable baseline with predictable patches, auditable artifacts, and controlled risk.

This doc covers:
- Required reviewers and sign-offs for LTS backports
- Risk scoring and what changes are eligible for LTS
- Release Manager responsibilities for LTS patch releases
- Exceptions/waivers and how they are documented
- Emergency security patch handling (including accelerated approvals)

## Scope
**In scope**
- All code changes landing on any Runtime LTS branch
- Release-branch CI/build/publish steps that affect LTS artifacts
- Policy for waiving gates (test/perf) for LTS releases

**Out of scope**
- Mainline (`main`) change management
- Long-term roadmap/feature planning (LTS prioritizes stability)
- Customer-specific hotfix forks (h...

text:
## Purpose
This page is an internal, customer-facing guidance playbook for Support, Customer Success, and Solutions Engineering. Use it to:
- Explain Redwood RBAC v2 clearly (least privilege + auditable authorization).
- Recommend standard role setups by persona (Hosted, Dedicated, Private).
- Troubleshoot “permission denied” (403) issues efficiently and consistently.
- Collect the right artifacts for escalation (without asking customers for sensitive data).

**Audience:** Support/CS/SE working with regulated enterprise customers (finance/healthcare/insurance) and Private/Dedicated pilots.

---

## RBAC v2 in one slide (customer-friendly framing)
**What it is:** RBAC v2 is Redwood’s fine-grained authorization layer. Every sensitive action maps to a specific permission string; roles are just named collections of permissions; users/service identities get access via role bindings at spec...

text:
Overview
This playbook consolidates Redwood Inference’s company-wide operational policies and reusable templates for: access & permissions, change management, data management, procurement & expenses, vendor management, travel, and internal templates. It is intended for people managers, engineers proposing changes, procurement requesters, security reviewers, and finance approvers. Use the checklists and templates in the final section when beginning a request.

Principles and governance
- Principle: Least privilege by default. Access is granted at the minimum level required and reviewed quarterly.
- Principle: Segregation of duties for procurement and change approvals where feasible.
- Principle: Data classification drives retention, access controls, and encryption requirements.
- Principle: Risk-based change management — low-risk operational changes may be expedited; high-risk, custome...

h1. Purpose
This policy defines what constitutes a production change at Redwood Inference and the minimum requirements to plan, approve, execute, and validate the change. The goal is to minimize customer impact while maintaining developer velocity.

h1. Scope
Applies to all production environments for the Hosted API and Console, including:
* Hosted API control plane and data plane services
* Console UI and backend
* Shared platform dependencies (routing, auth, billing metering, observability agents)

Out of scope: customer-managed environments for *Private deployments* (see the Private Deployment Change Policy), and vendor-managed changes outside Redwood’s control (cloud provider maintenance).

h1. Definitions
* *Production:* Any environment serving external customer traffic (including canary and regional production).

title: Emergency change policy (production)
preview:
h1. Policy statement
An *emergency change* is a production change required to mitigate an active or imminent customer-impacting incident. Emergency changes are allowed to bypass standard change windows and review timelines, but must meet the approval and documentation requirements below.

h1. Examples
* Emergency ro...
text:
h1. Policy statement
An *emergency change* is a production change required to mitigate an active or imminent customer-impacting incident. Emergency changes are allowed to bypass standard change windows and review timelines, but must meet the approval and documentation requirements below.

h1. Examples
* Emergency rollback of a Serving Runtime release
* Disabling a faulty feature flag causing elevated 5xx
* Routing traffic away from a degraded region

h1. Minimum required approvals
Emergency changes require at least one of:
* Incident Commander approval (for an active incident), OR
* SRE oncall approval (if no IC assigned yet)

Additional required approvals:
* If the change impacts *Dedicated* customers with premium SLAs: CS oncall or Account Owner must be notified as soon as practical.
* If the change touches authentication, RBAC, audit logging, or encryption: Security oncall must app...
